nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Herries
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  Norman, Scottish, English: locative name from Hérils in Trévières (Calvados). The name in England may have died out before the end of the medieval period, but survived into the modern period in Scotland.
2 English: variant of Herry with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. Compare Harris .

variants.name: Herris
meanings: English:  see  Herries  .

variants.name: Herry
meanings: Scots: Herry, to plunder, most properly to plunder birds' nests

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

nameMeanings.name: Lindsay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Lindsey in Lincolnshire, England. This is first found in the form Lindissi, apparently a derivative of the British name of Lincoln . To this was later added the Old English element ēg ‘island’, since the place was virtually cut off by the surrounding fenland. The surname was taken to Scotland at an early date and is the name of an important and powerful Scottish family.
Irish: adopted as an equivalent ofvarious Gaelic names (see   Lindsey  ).

variants.name: Linzy
meanings: Altered form of Scottish, Irish, or English   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  . Compare   Lenzy  .

variants.name: Linzey
meanings: English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .

variants.name: Lindsey
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lindsay  .
Irish: shortened and Anglicized form of various Gaelic surnames, as for example Ó Loingsigh (see Lynch 1), Mac Giolla Fhionntóg (see McClintock ), and Ó Fhloinn (see Flynn and Lynn ).

variants.name: Lincoln
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of Lincoln, so named from an original British name Lindo- ‘lake’ + Latin colonia ‘settlement, colony’. The place was an important administrative center during the Roman occupation of Britain and in the Middle Ages it was a center for the manufacture of cloth, including the famous ‘Lincoln green’.
African American: adoption of the surname in 1 above, in honor of Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the US, who abolished slavery.
History: Abraham Lincoln (1809–65), 16th president of the US, was the son of an illiterate laborer, descended from Samuel Lincoln, who had emigrated from England to MA in 1637.

variants.name: Linsey
meanings: Scottish, Irish, or English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .
Americanized form of German Linse or Swiss German Linsi, see Lins .

nameMeanings.name: Eufemia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Euphemia .

en.lifeSummary: Eufemia de Herries Lindsay was born in 1450, in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. She had at least 1 son with Sir Thomas Corry. She died in 1478, in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 28.

fr.lifeSummary: Eufemia de Herries Lindsay est née en 1450, à Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Écosse. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir Thomas Corry. Elle est décédée en 1478, à Ayr, Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 28 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Eufemia de Herries Lindsay wurde 1450 in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir Thomas Corry mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1478 im Alter von 28 Jahren in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Eufemia de Herries Lindsay è nata il nel 1450 nel Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir Thomas Corry. Morì nel 1478, nel Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, all'età di 28 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Eufemia de Herries Lindsay(은)는 1450년 스컷랜드 압르디실르 압르디에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir Thomas Corry(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1478년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 에이르에서 28세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Eufemia de Herries Lindsay nasceu em 1450, em Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir Thomas Corry. Ela faleceu em 1478, em Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, com 28 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Eufemia de Herries Lindsay родилась в 1450 г. в местности абирдин, абирдиншир, Шотландия. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir Thomas Corry. Она умерла в 1478 г. в местности аир, аиршир, Шотландия в возрасте 28 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Eufemia de Herries Lindsay nació en 1450, en Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sir Thomas Corry. Murió en 1478, en Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, a la edad de 28 años.
